quote: Because Rust compiles generic code into code that specifies the type in each instance, we pay no runtime cost for using generics. When the code runs, it performs just as it would if we had duplicated each definition by hand.
notes: Load-bearing for round-2 generics keep_essential; verdict stands.

quote: This lookup incurs a runtime cost that doesn't occur with static dispatch. Dynamic dispatch also prevents the compiler from choosing to inline a method's code, which in turn prevents some optimizations.
notes: All substantive elements confirmed verbatim; only the dyn Trait phrasing is ours, not the page wording.

quote: Generics are not compatible with vtables. / It must not have any associated constants. / Not have an opaque return type.
notes: Page adds further restrictions (generic associated types, supertrait dyn-compatibility, AsyncFn family) that strengthen, not contradict, the card.

quote: Specialized generic types are created one time for each unique value type that is used as a parameter. / every time that a constructed type is instantiated with a reference type as its parameter, regardless of what type it is, the runtime reuses the previously created specialized version. This is possible because all references are the same size. / reflection can query it at run time and both its actual type and its type parameter can be ascertained.

Attempt to convert calls to virtual functions to direct calls. / Inline also indirect calls that are discovered to be known at compile time thanks to previous inlining. / all the function bodies are read from these ELF sections and instantiated as if they had been part of the same translation unit.
notes: All four named options confirmed verbatim.

quote: Safe Rust guarantees an absence of data races... A data race has Undefined Behavior, and is therefore impossible to perform in Safe Rust. / Data races are prevented mostly through Rust's ownership system alone: it's impossible to alias a mutable reference, so it's impossible to perform a data race. / However Rust does not prevent general race conditions.
notes: FINDING: the originally cited send-and-sync page describes mechanisms but leaves the data-race link as an explicit TODO. Claim is true but required nomicon/races.html as the authoritative source. Card amended: added source; scope sharpened — data races prevented, general race conditions not (and cannot violate memory safety alone in safe code).

quote: After escape analysis, the server compiler eliminates the scalar replaceable object allocations and the associated locks from generated code. / It does not replace a heap allocation with a stack allocation for objects that do not globally escape.

The distinct_objects operation takes a list of memrefs and returns the same memrefs, with the additional assumption that accesses to them will never alias with each other. / The inbounds flag is always emitted (valid since indices are guaranteed in-bounds) and causes undefined behavior if that precondition is violated.

A linalg.generic op fully derives the specification of its iteration space from its operands. / fully declares the type of its iterators. / The following key transformations have been central to driving the design of Linalg: ... Parametric Tiling ... Promotion ... Fusion ... Vectorization ... Lower to Loops ... Lower to Library Calls.

the algorithm is specified once at the top, separately from the optimizations. / if you write a Halide function with an update definition (see lesson 9), then it is not safe to evaluate the same point multiple times.
notes: Confirmed; update-definition caveat already on the card.
